name: Eggplants with Labaneh
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the eggplants without peeling them, about ½ inch thick.
2. Place the slices in a colander and sprinkle generously with salt (coarse sea salt is best, but any salt will do). Allow the bitter liquid to drain out.
3. After one hour, rinse the eggplant slices under running water.
4. Dry the slices thoroughly with a clean towel.
5. Dredge each slice in flour, then deep fry until golden brown in oil.
6. Place the fried slices on a serving plate or baking paper, arranging them side by side.
7. When the plate is full, add prepared Labaneh and spread it evenly over the eggplant slices as if frosting a cake, leaving an empty space in the center.
8. In a small pan, heat olive oil until hot but not smoking. Add pine seeds and cook just until lightly browned, taking care not to burn them. Remove from heat and distribute the pine seeds and a few drops of oil over the eggplant slices with Labaneh.
9. Serve immediately.
This delightful appetizer serves 2 slices per person and is perfect for any number of guests as they arrive.
